title: Establishment of the DAO Casters Program — Elected Content Creators for Star Atlas Ecosystem Promotion
brief: This proposal introduces the DAO Casters—a structured program of five elected content creators dedicated to promoting the entire Star Atlas ecosystem, including the DAO, the game, and broader community initiatives. DAO Casters will be elected through a formal election process (self-nomination, 1 STV round) as a once off measure (any further extension of this program would require a new proposal). Each Caster will receive a monthly stipend of 360,000 ATLAS, plus an additional 360,000 ATLAS per month to fund giveaways, contests, and promotions.  Caster responsibilities will include weekly content, monthly community livestreams, and regular collaborations. This program provides the ecosystem with consistent, engaging, and professional media representation.
description:
# 1. Establishment of the DAO Casters Program — Elected Content Creators for Star Atlas Ecosystem Promotion
- Author: Signal
- Date: March 31, 2025
- Type: Community Engagement

---

# 2. Summary
This proposal introduces the DAO Casters—a structured program of five elected content creators dedicated to promoting the entire Star Atlas ecosystem, including the DAO, the game, and broader community initiatives. DAO Casters will be elected through a formal election process (self-nomination, 1 STV round) as a once off measure (any further extension of this program would require a new proposal). Each Caster will receive a monthly stipend of 360,000 ATLAS, plus an additional 360,000 ATLAS per month to fund giveaways, contests, and promotions.  Caster responsibilities will include weekly content, monthly community livestreams, and regular collaborations. This program provides the ecosystem with consistent, engaging, and professional media representation. 

## **Total cost for this program is 10,800,000 ATLAS covering 3 months of activity.** 

---

# 3. Motivation
While the Star Atlas Council focuses on governance and strategic development, the broader ecosystem lacks a consistently visible, engaging public presence. Without a dedicated promotional effort, many aspects of the Star Atlas experience—from its game universe to its governance opportunities—remain underutilized and under-promoted. Community creators have long expressed a desire to help grow the ecosystem but lack a formal structure and support to do so effectively.

The DAO Casters program addresses this by empowering a team of skilled, elected creators and promoters to be ambassadors not just for the DAO, but for the entire Star Atlas brand. This includes publicizing ecosystem developments, promoting active community engagement, creating educational or hype-driven content, and running creative campaigns that make Star Atlas more accessible and exciting to both current and future participants.

---

# 4. Specification
## Definitions
- DAO Caster: An elected content creator or marketer / promoter representing the Star Atlas ecosystem, including the DAO, game, and community.
- Promotional Budget: A separate ATLAS-denominated allocation for each Caster, intended solely for giveaways, contests, or other community-engagement initiatives.

## Role Description
Each DAO Caster will:
- Be elected for 3 months through a formal DAO vote (self-nomination, 1 STV round).
- Produce at least one piece of original content per week (e.g., video, podcast, thread, stream, article, promotion, meme).
- Co-host a monthly DAO Casters Brew livestream on the Star Atlas Discord, serving as a relaxed but interactive touchpoint with the community and team.
- Collaborate with at least one other DAO Caster per month to co-create a joint piece of content.
- Receive 360,000 ATLAS/month as personal compensation.
- Have earmarked 360,000 ATLAS/month as a promotional budget to support engagement campaigns, contests, or audience-building activities.  A budgeted and detailed promotional schedule has to be submitted and approved by the DAO Council for these funds to be released each month.

DAO Casters will coordinate among themselves to schedule Brew sessions based on their time zones and availability. They will remain self-directed and personally accountable,  much like the Star Atlas DAO council.  Payments may be paused, withheld, or temporarily frozen on any DAO Caster if a complaint of non performance is submitted to the DAO Council and deemed to have merit.

---

# 5. Stakeholder Impact
## Community Members
- Gain access to consistent, high-quality, creative content that covers all aspects of Star Atlas: game development, governance, lore, and events.
- Have regular engagement opportunities through giveaways, campaigns, and interactive livestreams.
- Experience a more accessible and inviting on-ramp to the Star Atlas universe.

## Content Creators / Ecosystem Promoters
- Receive meaningful support and structure for contributing to the Star Atlas ecosystem.
- Gain visibility and recognition through an elected, compensated role.

## Council Members
- Are relieved of promotional responsibilities, allowing them to focus fully on governance, oversight, and treasury matters.

## Star Atlas Foundation
- Provides infrastructure and disburses funds, but does not direct content strategy—preserving creative independence while enabling execution.

---

# 6. Implementation Details
## Election Process
- Held once, for a 3 month term.
- Candidates self-nominate through a public application form hosted on the DAO’s forum or portal. Form and process to be created and hosted by Star Atlas council.
- Elections will use a single STV (Single Transferable Vote) round, identical to the Council election method.
- The top five vote recipients will be appointed as DAO Casters.
- In the event of a tie, a runoff vote will be initiated.

## Payout & Fund Management
- Monthly compensation is in ATLAS and paid out in lump sums at the end of each served month.
- 360,000 ATLAS/month/caster will be paid at the end of each served 30-day period as salary.
- 360,000 ATLAS/month/caster will be earmarked at the start of each 30 day period directly in ATLAS for promotional use (giveaways, contests, etc.).  Approved budgets and marketing campaigns needed for these funds to be released.
- All payments will be distributed by the Star Atlas Foundation to a DAO council 4:6 multi sig wallet. The council will coordinate individual payments to creators. A new PIP would be required to restart this program.

## Hosting & Infrastructure
- Monthly DAO Casters Brews will be hosted live on the Star Atlas Discord.
- Casters will coordinate internally to select monthly times that ensure full attendance across time zones.
- DAO Caster content will be published on their own channels but may be amplified through official DAO or Foundation media where appropriate.

---

# 7. Timeline
- Weeks 1–2 (Post-Approval): Finalize onboarding materials, announce the initiative, and prepare election infrastructure.
- Weeks 3–4: Open nominations and begin the first election cycle.
- Week 5: Voting concludes; the top five candidates are selected and onboarded.
- Month 2: DAO Casters release their first content, set the Brew calendar, and launch initial engagement efforts.  Payments begin.

---

# 8. Cost & Resource Allocation
## One-Time Costs
- None.

## Ongoing Costs
- Compensation (ATLAS): 360,000 ATLAS/month × 5 Casters = 1,800,000 ATLAS/month 
- Promotional Funds (ATLAS): 360,000 ATLAS/month × 5 Casters = 1,800,000 ATLAS/month
- **Total Monthly Equivalent: 3,600,000 ATLAS/month**

## Total Cost
* **Total cost for this program is 10,800,000 ATLAS covering 3 months of activity.**  
* Any unused or non distributed funds at the completion of this program will be returned to the Star Atlas DAO Ecosystem Fund treasury, to be used and repurposed for other future proposals.
* All funding provided by the Star Atlas Foundation.  Compensation may be withheld, stopped, or temporarily frozen if a complaint of non performance is issued and deemed to be valid by the DAO Council.

---

# 9. Additional Details
- Conflicts of Interest: I am a Council member. I filed this before being elected to council.  I’ve recused myself from duties tied to the review stage.
- Earlier Versions: None
